California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 28′  ■990
Honorab■e NiChO■as C. Petr■s
California State Senate
State Capito■
Sacramento′  CA  958■4
Re:  Your Request For AdV■Ce
Our Fi■e No. A-90-079
Dear senator Petris:
You have requested advice under the conflict of interest
disclosure proviiions of the Political 
Reform Act (the rraqg't1' 1/
The formal limited partnership of which you are one of the
owners should be disclosed on Schedule A (Investnents) of the Form
72l-. In addition, if you received incorne of $250 or more fron the
partnership during the calendar year, you must disclose the incorne
on schedule D.
Because your ownership interest in the partnership is less
than lot, you are not required to disclose the interests in real
property held by the partnership, and you are not required to
disclose the names of sources of income received by the business.
L/ covernment code sections 81ooo-91015. AII statutory
references are to the Governnent Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. AlI references to regulations ari to
Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code oi Regulations.
You own a 68 interest
of a dozen owners through a
The partnership has a name.
disc Iosed?
QUEST■ON
in a commercial bui■ding.  You are one
fo■■lal ■imited partnership agreement.
How shou■d this investment be
CONCLUSION

senator Nicholas Petris
Page Tuo
ANALYSIS
As an official designated in Section 872OO, the Act requires
that you file annual statements of economic interests (Form 72I).
The following nust be disclosed on your annual statements:
- On Schedule A, i.nvestments in which you had an ownership
interest of $1,o0o or more at any time during the calendar year;
- On schedule B, interests in real property which vou hoLd
directlv (i.e, not through a partnership or other business entity)
in which you had an ownership interest of $1,o0o or more at any
tiure during the calendar year r'
- On Schedules D and E, incone and loans of $250 or nore
during the calendar year received from a single source r'
- on Schedules F and G, gifts and honoraria received during
the calendar year with an aggregate value of $5o or more from a
single source;
- on schedule H-2, the narne of any source of incorne to a
business entitv (such as the partnership described in vour letter)
of which vour ownershio interest was Io* or more, if your pro-rata
share of the incorue received by the business entity from a single
source was $Io,000 or more during the caLendar year,' and
- on schedule H-3, the nane of any source of rental incorne
frorn real property vou own directlv (i.e., not through a business
entity such as a real estate lirnited partnership), if you received
$10,000 or nore in rental incone from the person during the
calendar year.
Sections 87206 and 87207 set out the infornation which roust
be disclosed for each reportable investnent, interest in real
property and source of income.
The question you have asked us to address in your letter
concerns disclosure of a fornal, f.inited partnership which owns a
commercial building, The Act,s definition of rr investmentrr
includes any financial interest in a business entity, including a
partnership. (Section A2O34.) When a formal partnership is
created, the partnership nust be disclosed on Schedule A.
In addition, if your ownership interest in any partnership is
10t or qreater, you nust disclose:


Senator Petris
Page Three
-- on Schedule C-1, any interests in real property or
investments held by the partnership if your pro rala oinership
interest in any holding is 91,O0O or noie;
-- On Schedule H-2, any source of incorne to the partnership,
of which your pro rata share of income paid by the soirrce to the
partnership was from any single source was $1O,OOO or more.
If your ownership interest in a partnership is less than I0*,
you are not required to disclose investnents and interests in real
property held by the partnership, and you are not required to
disclose any source of incone to the partnership.
In either case, if you receive $250 or more j,n incone froro
the partnership, you must disclose the incone on Schedule D.
In our telephone conversation, we discussed whether a
I'business entity, tt in the forn of a partnership, is created by
virtue of shared ownership of real property, Uut for which no
partnership or other busj.ness entity is formally created. I{hen
two or nore individuals own a piece of real property, a rrbusiness
entitytr exists, even though no formal partneiship il created.
(See Section 82005.) In such a situation, an I investmentr in the
business entity may be disclosed on Schedule A of the Form 721.2/
You have pointed out that, in past years, you have received
conflicting advice from commission staff concerning the proper
nethod of disclosing such partnerships. It is true that
Cornrnission staff generally has advised in the past that when an
official 
owns a portion of an interest in real property, and no
fornal partnership or other business entity is created, the
interest in real property nay be disclosed- on Schedule B. In the
case of an ownership interest of less than IOt, this nethod of
disclosure actually results in more discrosure because infornation
concerning the location of the real property, as teII as the value
of the official,s 
share, are disclosed.
2/ Because afr".. ,iff -be no legal name for the partnership, a
descriptive term should used foi rName of Busineis Entity.; 
For
example, a partnership between yourself and five others rnigtrt re
calIed rrRear Estate venture A, Nicholas petris and Five paitners.r'
The rrDescription of Business Activityrt night be 'rownership of
conmercial building. r The rvaluer would be deternined by
nultiplying your ovrnership interest in the partnership iirnes ttre
value of the property held by the partnersh-i.p


senator Nicholas Petris
Page Four
The commission wilL be asked to consider a regulation
defining the term irbusiness entityrt for purposes of conflict of
interest disclosure under the Act. Until the regTulation is
adopted, we believe it is appropriate to disclose interests in
real property which are owned jointly with other people, but for
which no formal partnership or other business entitv has been
created, in either method described above. That is:
(1) A partnership roay disclosed on schedule A and, if the
official's 
ownership interest in the partnership is 10t or
greater, each interest in real property owned by the partnership,
of which the official's 
pro-rata share is $1,000 or more, Dust be
disclosed on Schedule c-1. 
Schedule H-2 must also be conpleted if
the official's 
pro-rata share of incone received by the
partnership is $I0,ooo or more fron a singLe source. (rn this
case, the real property is NoT reported on Schedule B.)
OR
(21 The interests in real property may be disclosed on
schedule B, and the official's 
pro-rata share of incoroe received
from each interest must be reported on Schedule D, if the
official's 
pro-rata share is 9250 or more. In addition, if any
one renter makes palments of which the official's 
pro rata share
is $t0,ooo or more, the nane of the renter must be disclosed on
Schedule H-3. (In this case, the partnership is NOT reported on
Schedule A, and Schedules c-I and c-2 do not apply.)
I hope this letter adequately answers your questions. If you
have additional questions, please call me at (9I5) 322-5662.
s incerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General counsel
By:
(\ o-a-,,-.a, /llr-te-t-r^.1
dAnn Pritchard c,
Division Chief
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division
